name: Arch
description: Archaerhodopsin-3 (Arch) was the first opsin-based GEVI to demonstrate optical recording of single action potentials in mammalian neurons, achieving sub-millisecond response times but with a very low quantum yield.

researchPapers.title: Enhanced archaerhodopsin fluorescent protein voltage indicators
researchPapers.authors: Gong Y, Li JZ, Schnitzer MJ
researchPapers.sample: Cultured rat hippocampal neurons, in vitro

researchPapers.title: Archaerhodopsin voltage imaging: synaptic calcium and BK channels stabilize action potential repolarization at the Drosophila neuromuscular junction
Archaerhodopsin variants with enhanced voltage-sensitive fluorescence in mammalian and Caenorhabditis elegans neurons
researchPapers.authors: Flytzanis NC, Bedbrook CN, Chiu H, Engqvist MKM, Xiao C, Chan KY, Sternberg PW, Arnold FH, Gradinaru V
researchPapers.sample: Cultured rat hippocampal neurons and C. elegans neurons, in vitro/in vivo
